name: Dock
notes: Engineer-built, standalone — placed on the Plains hex adjoining an Ocean or River tile, not on the water tile itself (see game-design/01-map-and-terrain.md), same siteTerrain/adjacentTerrainRequired shape as Port. Allows ship landing only — no production. Naval troops can only disembark onto land at a Dock or a Port/Shipyard, nowhere else along a coast/riverbank. Public infrastructure once built — usable by any player's troops, not just the builder's owner. Destructible by any troop, but only after clearing defenders on/near it first; deletes outright on destruction (no ruin), freeing the hex for a full-cost fresh build rather than a discounted ruin-rebuild — rebuildCost does not apply. Not tied to a base, so it doesn't consume a population slot and isn't gated by any HQ level. Buildable in Stone or Wood: Wood is cheaper but weaker and Fire-vulnerable, consistent with the Wood-tier vulnerability used across Wall/Tower/Bridge.
